rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Petition For Reconsideration Of Termination Sanctions And Reconsideration Of Dismissal Of Operative Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 23, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SALMA KHAN'S Motion And Petition For Reconsideration Of Termination Sanctions And Reconsideration Of Dismissal Of Operative Complaint. The motion is denied. The court lacks the authority to hear this motion for reconsideration because a judgment was entered on May 19, 2015. See Magallanes v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 167 Cal.App.3d 878, 882; Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236-1238. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LOURDES ESCALANTE VS. DEWEY DONALD SELPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Motion To Compel Responses To Third Set Of Special Interrogatories Related Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday June 23, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT DEWEY SELPH, HEARST COMMUNICATIONS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Third Set Of Special Interrogatories and Related Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Compel Responses to Third Set of Special Interrogatories and Related Request for Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants served the subject interrogatories in September 2014, and they have not yet been answered. Plaintiff's counsel's justification - that Plaintiff is "impossible to get a hold of and pin down to help with the discovery" is inadequate, especially where discovery has been outstanding for such a long period of time. Accordingly, the tentative ruling is to GRANT the motion and to impose sanctions against Plaintiff and her counsel in the total amount of $1,860. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX REL. DENNIS VS. LILAY, INC D/B/A INSTANT TAX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 23, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS LILAY, INC D/B/A INSTANT TAX, AND KEBRON DANIEL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied and the demurrer is overruled. The complaint must contain: a statement of facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language. (Code Civ. Proc., § 425.10, subd. (a).) "While it is true that pleading conclusions of law does not fulfill this requirement, it has long been recognized that '[t]he distinction between conclusions of law and ultimate facts is not at all clear and involves at most a matter of degree. [Citations.] For example, the courts have permitted allegations which obviously included conclusions of law and have termed them 'ultimate facts' or 'conclusions of fact.' (Burks v. Poppy Construction Co. (1962) 57 Cal.2d 463, 473 [20 Cal.Rptr. 609, 370 P.2d 313].) What is important is that the complaint as a whole contain sufficient facts to apprise the defendant of the basis upon which the plaintiff is seeking relief." (Perkins v. Superior Court, (1981) 117 Cal. App. 3d 1, 6.) Here, the paragraphs that are the subject of Defendant's objections are appropriate to apprise Defendant of the basis upon which Plaintiff is seeking relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ARCHIBALD CUNNINGHAM VS. KEVIN SINGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kevin Singer, Ed Deleski Joinder In Motion Of Defendants Coombs And Woods For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 23, 2015, Line 14, Defendants Kevin Singer and Ed Deleski's Joinder In Motion Of Defendants Coombs And Woods For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security. Granted. Pursuant to CCP sec. 391.1, Plaintiff must furnish security in the amount of $75,000 in order to pursue this action. The evidence submitted in support of the motion establishes that Plaintiff is a vexatious litigant and that there is not a reasonable probability that he will prevail in the litigation against Defendants. Plaintiff's argument in opposition that CCP § 391.1 does not apply to represented litigants is unavailing. In Shalant v. Girardi (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1164, 1173, the case cited by Plaintiff, the Court states, "If, as here, the plaintiff is able to file the action through counsel, sections 391.1 to 391.6 continue to provide a remedy against the plaintiff's unjustifiable behavior in the pending case." Thus, even though Plaintiff is represented by counsel, CCP sec. 391.1 still applies. The Court did not consider the "Response" filed by Plaintiff on June 15, 2015, as Plaintiff was not granted leave of Court to file an additional brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GIBBS INTERNATIONAL, INC. VS. CALIFORNIA FIRST, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 23, 2015, Line 17, Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. The court denies plaintiff's application for preliminary injunction. First, plaintiff's showing of imminent and irreparable harm is tenuous. Plaintiff is seeking money damages and it is unclear when the settlement proceeds will be paid to the defendants. (Boozer dec., par. 11). Second, it is dubious whether plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary of the Facilitation Agreement or its amendment. The contract merely provides that the funds will be placed with Urban Title Co., Inc. It does not provide that the placement is necessarily for plaintiff's benefit. Third, plaintiff has an adequate remedy in the New York or South Carolina forums. Those forums apparently have jurisdiction over the Antarctica entities. The thrust of this action is to prevent the Antarctica entities from dissipating the settlement funds and leaving plaintiff without a remedy or with a hollow judgment. The New York or South Carolina courts can restrain the Antarctica entities from dissipating the funds. Principles of comity weigh in favor of denying this motion. Fourth, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence showing alter ego or enterprise liability and that argument is improperly raised for the first time in the reply brief. Lastly, the balance of equities and harms weighs in defendants' favor. Based on the instant record, defendants have no interest in the funds that are supposed to go to the Antarctica entities or plaintiff and defendants should not be embroiled in this satellite litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
